sections.subtitle: Team Standings and Current Form
sections.text: Villarreal, currently sitting in 5th place in the league with 61 points, are in strong form with a streak of 3 consecutive wins. Meanwhile, Leganés, positioned at 18th with 34 points, have managed to avoid defeat in their last 4 matches, showing resilience despite their lower standing. This matchup at Estadio de la Cerámica in Villarreal presents a clear contrast between a top-tier side and a team fighting for survival. Villarreal's offensive prowess, with 61 goals scored this season ranking 3rd in the league, will be a major test for Leganés, who have struggled defensively.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 encounters, Villarreal have dominated with 4 wins compared to Leganés’ single victory. The most recent clash on December 22, 2024, saw Villarreal thrash Leganés 5-2 away from home, with Thierno Barry earning Player of the Match honors with an exceptional 9.4 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head stats also show a trend of high-scoring games, with 6 out of 7 matches featuring over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in 5 of those 7 games. Villarreal have been the first to score in 5 of their last 6 meetings, highlighting their ability to take early control.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Villarreal
sections.text: Villarreal boast several standout players, including Alejandro Baena, who ranks 1st in the league for key passes with 91 and 3rd for big chances created with 19, alongside 8 assists, placing him 3rd in the league for assists. Gerard Moreno, a consistent performer against Leganés with 4 goals in the last 5 matchups and an average Sofascore rating of 7.5, also ranks 2nd league-wide for aerial duels won at 81%. Ayoze Pérez, with 16 goals and an overperformance of 3.927 against his expected goals of 12.07, will be a major threat upfront. Additionally, Nicolas Pépé, in form with a 7.78 average rating over the last 5 matches, adds depth to their attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Leganés
sections.text: Leganés will rely on Dani Raba, their top performer with 7 goals and 6 assists, alongside a solid 7.19 average Sofascore rating, to spark their offense. Juan Cruz and Munir El Haddadi, each with 4 goals, have shown capability in front of goal, overperforming their expected goals significantly with 2.3984 and 2.7184 respectively. However, with no players currently in standout form over the last 5 matches, Leganés might struggle to match Villarreal’s firepower. Diego Garcia, a forward with 3 goals, could also play a role if given the chance to exploit defensive gaps.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Stats
sections.text: Villarreal’s strength lies in their attacking metrics, ranking 3rd in the league for total goals scored (61), big chances created (104), and accurate crosses (187). They also dominate in headers scored with 12 (1st in the league) and excel in duels, ranking 2nd in aerial duels won at 54.93% and 3rd in ground duels won at 52.35%. Leganés, on the other hand, have a concerning defensive record, conceding regularly as seen in their streak of 3 games without a clean sheet and both teams scoring in 7 of their last 9 away matches. Villarreal’s ability to create fast breaks, ranking 2nd with 1.4 per game, could exploit Leganés’ vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Goal Distribution and Timing
sections.text: Villarreal tend to score across various periods, with 14 goals each in the 31-45 and 76-90 minute ranges, accounting for roughly 17% and 19% of their total goals respectively. Leganés, however, show a tendency to score late, with 10 goals in the 76-90 minute period making up 37.7% of their total, suggesting they might push for a late comeback if trailing. Villarreal’s ability to strike early, as seen with 4 out of 5 recent matches where they scored first, could be crucial in dictating the game’s tempo from the outset.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Disciplinary Expectations
sections.text: The match will be officiated by Ricardo De Burgos Bengoetxea, who averages 4.7 yellow cards per game and 0.19 red cards per game, indicating a likelihood of a physical encounter with frequent bookings. Villarreal have accumulated 92 yellow cards this season, ranking 16th for least yellow cards, while averaging 2.63 per game, suggesting they may contribute to the referee’s busy day. With Leganés also known for physical play, especially in away fixtures, expect a game with several stoppages due to fouls.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Villarreal will miss key forward Thierno Barry, who scored 11 goals this season and was pivotal in their last meeting against Leganés, alongside other absences like Ilias Akhomach and Raúl Albiol, potentially weakening their depth. Leganés, missing defender Enric Franquesa, might face challenges in containing Villarreal’s wing play, especially with players like Alejandro Baena creating big chances. Both teams will need to adapt, but Villarreal’s stronger squad depth could give them an edge despite these absences.
